title: Dutch Malty Dutch Beef Bitterballen
description: A traditional Dutch dish featuring beef, malts, and spices, coated with a flavorful binchotan charcoal mixture. This energizing and low-carb-friendly dish is perfect for a snack or light meal.

ingredients.name: malted barley flour
ingredients.notes: Use a high-carb malted barley flour for authenticity

ingredients.name: low-carb all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: Add crunch and structure to the bitterballen

ingredients.name: annatto powder
ingredients.notes: Ground annatto for a vibrant yellow color and natural flavor

ingredients.name: chopped onion
ingredients.notes: Sautéed and caramelized for added sweetness

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Unrefined sea salt for maximum flavor

ingredients.name: binchotan charcoal powder
ingredients.notes: Activated charcoal for a crispy coating

instructions: Combine ground beef, malted barley flour, low-carb all-purpose flour, annatto powder, chopped onion, garlic powder, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Mix well with your hands until just combined.
Divide the mixture into 12 equal portions and shape into small oval shapes.
Dredge each bitterballen in binchotan charcoal powder to coat evenly.
Heat about 2-3 inches of oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
When the oil reaches 170°C, carefully place 4-6 bitterballen into the oil and fry for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the bitterballen from the oil with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining bitterballen.
Serve the Dutch Malty Dutch Beef Bitterballen warm and enjoy!
